tasks.subject: [devtools-bridge] [P1] Inject in-page capture shim + server ingest + per-session buffer
tasks.description:
Build the server half of the capture pipeline: inject an in-page capture shim into workbench-served/proxied HTML, ingest what the client relays, and hold it in a bounded per-session buffer. No agent-facing tool yet — verified through the store in tests.

FILES:
- NEW apps/server/src/services/workbench-serve/devtools-inject.ts: export `DEVTOOLS_AGENT_SCRIPT` (a self-contained, dependency-free IIFE string) and `injectDevtoolsScript(html: string): string`. The shim: (a) on load postMessage({__dorkosDevtools:'hello'}) to window.parent and buffer locally until the parent acks (self-suppress when embedded elsewhere); (b) wrap console.log/info/warn/error/debug preserving passthrough, serializing args circular-safe + depth/size-capped, attaching .stack for errors; (c) window 'error' + 'unhandledrejection' listeners capturing message/filename/line/col/stack; (d) wrap window.fetch and XMLHttpRequest to record {method,url,status,ok,startedAt,durationMs,responseSize?,initiator} — NEVER request/response bodies; (e) batch on a ~250ms debounce and postMessage({__dorkosDevtools:'batch',console,network,seq}) to window.parent with monotonic seq; (f) on pagehide/beforeunload post {__dorkosDevtools:'navigated'}. Leave a documented seam for the screenshot 'capture-request' handler added in 3.1. `injectDevtoolsScript` inserts `<script>…</script>` as the FIRST <head> child (mirror mcp-apps/lib/sandbox.ts buildSandboxSrcDoc insertion: handle `<head …>` with attributes, `<html>`-only docs, and head-less fragments), inline (NOT `<script src>` — the opaque frame would fetch it cross-origin and CSP script-src blocks it more often).
- EDIT apps/server/src/routes/workbench-serve.ts handleServe: when the resolved file's content-type is text/html, read it to a string, run injectDevtoolsScript, send with recomputed Content-Length. Non-HTML responses keep streaming byte-for-byte unchanged.
- EDIT apps/server/src/services/workbench-serve/proxy.ts proxyToLocalhost: when upstream content-type is text/html, buffer the (small) HTML body, inject, relay with recomputed length; all other content-types stream unchanged. Injection composes with the existing frame-ancestors stripping.
- NEW apps/server/src/services/session/devtools-capture-store.ts: a Map<sessionId, CaptureBuffer> with bounded ring buffers (console 500, network 200, screenshot latest-1). CaptureBuffer = {console, network, screenshot|null, documentId, logicalUrl, updatedAt}. Chosen over per-turn session.uiState-style storage because capture is continuous ACROSS turns. API: append(sessionId, batch), setScreenshot(sessionId, requestId, entry), get(sessionId), markNavigated(sessionId), drop(sessionId). Wire drop() into session-close teardown.
- NEW apps/server/src/routes/session-devtools.ts: POST /api/sessions/:id/devtools/ingest — Zod-validate the batch, append to the session's rings; session-gated (credentialed client call); per-batch entry cap → 413 on overflow; 400 on malformed body. Mount under the session router.
- EDIT packages/shared/src/schemas.ts: DevtoolsIngestSchema, ConsoleEntrySchema, NetworkEntrySchema (+ exported types). Export via the appropriate @dorkos/shared subpath.
- EDIT apps/server/src/config/constants.ts WORKBENCH: CONSOLE_BUFFER=500, NETWORK_BUFFER=200, CAPTURE_DEBOUNCE_MS=250, INGEST_MAX_BATCH (cap), and screenshot dims/timeout used later.

SECURITY (mandatory): the injected shim talks ONLY to window.parent via postMessage — it must NEVER call /api/*. The sandbox string (WORKBENCH_SANDBOX_ISOLATED, no allow-same-origin) is UNCHANGED. Injection only ever touches text/html bodies.

TESTS (each with a purpose comment):
- injectDevtoolsScript: inserts before the first <head> child; handles `<HEAD>` uppercase, `<head data-x>` with attrs, `<html>`-only docs, head-less fragments, doctype-only preamble; leaves non-HTML untouched.
- proxy: text/html upstream buffered+injected+length-recomputed; non-HTML streams unchanged; injection composes with frame-ancestors stripping.
- store: ring eviction at caps (501st console entry drops the 1st); per-session isolation (session B never sees session A); markNavigated segmentation; drop on close.
- ingest route: Zod rejection → 400; oversize batch → 413; session-gated (401 when auth enabled, no creds); unknown session handled.
- shim pure helpers (arg serializer): circular-safe, depth/size-capped, preserves stack traces.
- REGRESSION: a test asserting the sandbox string is still WORKBENCH_SANDBOX_ISOLATED and the shim source contains no fetch('/api or XMLHttpRequest to /api.
tasks.activeForm: Building server-side capture injection, ingest, and buffer

tasks.subject: [devtools-bridge] [P1] Client bridge hook: relay postMessage captures to the ingest endpoint
tasks.description:
Build the client half of the capture pipeline: receive the shim's postMessage batches from the browser iframe and relay them to the ingest endpoint, scoped to the attached session.

FILES:
- NEW apps/client/src/layers/features/canvas/model/use-devtools-bridge.ts: a hook taking the iframe ref + documentId + logical url. Registers a window 'message' listener that accepts ONLY events where event.source === iframeRef.contentWindow (origin is 'null' for the opaque frame, so source-identity is the check — same discipline as the mcp-apps bridge). On {__dorkosDevtools:'hello'} → ack the child and begin relaying. Coalesce incoming console/network batches on a debounce and POST them via a new transport method, tagged {documentId, logicalUrl, console, network, seq}. Relay ONLY for the operator's attached session (reuse the existing attachedSessionId guard that gates ui_command delivery) so one session's preview never feeds another's buffer. Handle {__dorkosDevtools:'navigated'} by signaling a navigation marker to the store (via the ingest call or a dedicated field). Leave a documented seam for the screenshot capture-request handling wired in 3.1.
- EDIT apps/client/src/layers/features/canvas/ui/CanvasBrowserContent.tsx: mount use-devtools-bridge at the existing DevTools-bridge seam (the component already carries the seam comment). Pass the iframe ref (add a ref to the BrowserBody iframe), documentId, and currentUrl. No structural/behavioral change to navigation, chrome, or the sandbox — the browser looks and behaves exactly as v1.
- EDIT packages/shared/src/transport.ts + both impls: add `ingestDevtoolsCapture(sessionId, payload)` (POSTs to /api/sessions/:id/devtools/ingest). HttpTransport implements the fetch; DirectTransport no-ops (the browser is web-only already). TSDoc on the interface method.
- OPTIONAL (nice-to-have, cut if it risks the task): a subtle "DevTools connected" dot/badge in the browser chrome once the handshake completes — honest transparency that the agent can see this page. No new controls.

TESTS (RTL + mock Transport, each with a purpose comment):
- the hook accepts only event.source === contentWindow messages and REJECTS a foreign/nested-frame post (the core anti-spoofing guarantee — must be able to fail).
- batches are coalesced and relayed via the mock transport's ingest method with the right shape.
- relay happens only for the attached session (a non-attached session posts nothing).
- the handshake ack is sent on 'hello'.
- CanvasBrowserContent still renders the iframe with the unchanged sandbox flags (regression).
tasks.activeForm: Wiring the client bridge relay to the ingest endpoint

tasks.phaseName: P2 — Agent read tools (the self-verification core)
tasks.subject: [devtools-bridge] [P2] browser_read_console + browser_read_network MCP tools
tasks.description:
Expose the captured console/network to the agent as data-returning, session-bound, in-process MCP tools — the exact shape of the existing get_ui_state tool. After this task the edit→preview→read-own-errors→fix loop is live.

FILES:
- NEW apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/mcp-tools/devtools-tools.ts: export getDevtoolsTools(deps, session?) mirroring getUiTools in mcp-tools/ui-tools.ts. Session-bound handlers read the per-session CaptureBuffer (from devtools-capture-store, resolved by the bound session's id). Session-less variant returns a clear error result (mirror SESSIONLESS_UI_ERROR), never a fabricated success.
  - browser_read_console: input {level?: 'error'|'warn'|'info'|'log'|'debug'|'all', limit?: number}. Returns {documentUrl, capturedAt, entries: ConsoleEntry[], truncated, note?}. Honor level filter + limit.
  - browser_read_network: input {status?: 'all'|'failed', limit?: number}. Returns {documentUrl, capturedAt, requests: NetworkEntry[], truncated, note?}. Honor status filter + limit.
  Tool DESCRIPTIONS follow chrome-devtools-mcp naming/wording conventions and the writing-for-humans plain-language bar; state that they read the workbench browser preview for the ATTACHED session, and that they are empty when no local preview is open.
  STALENESS / NO-DOC: when the buffer is absent (no workbench browser document open, or the open page can't be instrumented — external sites and strict-CSP pages), return {entries/requests: [], note: 'No workbench browser document is currently open, or the open page can\'t be instrumented (external sites and pages with a strict CSP are not captured). Ask the user to open a local preview with browser_navigate.'}. capturedAt/documentUrl let the agent judge freshness.
- EDIT apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/mcp-tools/index.ts: register getDevtoolsTools alongside getUiTools (pass the per-query session through).
- EDIT apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/tooling/tool-filter.ts: add mcp__dorkos__browser_read_console and mcp__dorkos__browser_read_network to the allowlist (mirror the mcp__dorkos__get_ui_state entry).
- CODEX: deliberately NOT exposed. Add a short module-doc note in devtools-tools.ts explaining why, mirroring the get_ui_state exclusion rationale in codex-ui-mcp-server.ts: the Codex external MCP server is session-less, so a read tool there cannot resolve which session's buffer to read, and — unlike the fire-and-forget control_ui write whose effect the event-mapper injects downstream — a read tool must return data IN its result, which a session-less stub cannot produce. (browser_navigate already works on both runtimes.) Do NOT register these on codex-ui-mcp-server.

TESTS (each with a purpose comment; mirror the get_ui_state tests + FakeAgentRuntime):
- returns buffered console/network entries for a session whose store has data.
- honors level/status/limit filters and sets truncated correctly.
- returns the no-doc note when the buffer is empty.
- returns the session-less error when the tool is created without a bound session.
- the two tool ids are present in the tool-filter allowlist.
- the tools are NOT registered on the Codex UI MCP server.
tasks.activeForm: Building the console/network agent read tools

tasks.subject: [devtools-bridge] [P3] browser_screenshot via on-demand in-page rasterization round-trip
tasks.description:
Add the screenshot capability: the agent gets a rendered PNG of the current preview, captured on demand inside the opaque-origin frame (the parent cannot canvas-read it) and returned as MCP image content. This chunk is independently cuttable to a follow-up without blocking the console/network core.

ARCHITECTURE (recommended — on-demand round-trip; fallback = cadence-capture per spec §5 / Assumption A4):
1. browser_screenshot tool handler pushes a `devtools_capture_request` StreamEvent ({requestId}) onto session.eventQueue (+ notify) — the same mechanism control_ui uses to reach the client.
2. Client (attached session) receives it over SSE, forwards {__dorkosDevtools:'capture-request', requestId} to the frame, awaits {__dorkosDevtools:'capture-result', requestId, dataUrl|error}, then POSTs it to the ingest endpoint tagged with requestId (setScreenshot on the store).
3. The tool handler awaits the matching screenshot landing in the store (per-requestId resolver or short poll) with a timeout (~8s). On timeout / no preview → structured note, never a hang.

FILES:
- EDIT apps/server/src/services/workbench-serve/devtools-inject.ts (the shim): add the 'capture-request' handler — lazy `import('html-to-image')`, rasterize document.documentElement to a PNG data URL (dimension-capped via WORKBENCH constants), post {__dorkosDevtools:'capture-result', requestId, dataUrl}; on failure post {..., error}. html-to-image is imported INSIDE the shim only — never the main client bundle.
- EDIT packages/shared/src/schemas.ts: DevtoolsCaptureRequest StreamEvent variant ({requestId}); ScreenshotEntry + the ingest payload's screenshot branch (requestId + dataUrl).
- EDIT apps/server/src/services/runtimes/claude-code/mcp-tools/devtools-tools.ts: add browser_screenshot (input {}). Returns MCP image content (PNG) + {documentUrl, capturedAt}, or a structured no-preview/timeout note. Add to tool-filter allowlist (mcp__dorkos__browser_screenshot). Codex still excluded.
- EDIT apps/client/src/layers/features/canvas/model/use-devtools-bridge.ts: subscribe to the devtools_capture_request SSE event for the attached session, forward capture-request to the frame, relay capture-result to the ingest endpoint with its requestId.
- EDIT apps/server/src/routes/session-devtools.ts + store: accept the screenshot branch (setScreenshot(sessionId, requestId, entry)); resolve any awaiting tool handler.
- EDIT apps/server/src/config/constants.ts WORKBENCH: SCREENSHOT_MAX_WIDTH/HEIGHT, SCREENSHOT_TIMEOUT_MS.
- ADD dependency: html-to-image (only reachable via the lazy import in the shim).

TESTS (each with a purpose comment):
- browser_screenshot resolves and returns image content when a matching ingest lands.
- times out cleanly with a structured note when no capture-result arrives.
- returns the no-preview note when no browser document is open.
- the capture-request StreamEvent is pushed onto the session event queue.
- client bridge (RTL): forwards a capture-request to the frame and relays the result to the ingest transport method (stub html-to-image / a fake capture-result).
- the shim lazy-imports html-to-image only on a capture-request (no eager load).
